records.ety: [AS. þorn; akin to OS. & OFries. thorn, D. doorn, G. dorn, Dan. torn, Sw. törne, Icel. þorn, Goth. þarnus; cf. Pol. tarn, Russ. tern' the blackthorn, ternie thorns, Skr. ta grass, blade of grass. √53.]

defs: A hard and sharp-pointed projection from a woody stem; usually, a branch so transformed; a spine.
Any shrub or small tree which bears thorns; especially, any species of the genus Crataegus, as the hawthorn, whitethorn, cockspur thorn.
Fig.: That which pricks or annoys as a thorn; anything troublesome; trouble; care.
quotes: There was given to me a thorn in the flesh, the messenger of Satan to buffet me.
The guilt of empire, all its thorns and cares, Be only mine.

defs: The name of the Anglo-Saxon letter <?/, capital form <?/. It was used to represent both of the sounds of English th, as in thin, then. So called because it was the initial letter of thorn, a spine.
To prick, as with a thorn.
quotes: I am the only rose of all the stock That never thorn'd him.
